content.text:
The bug has been successfully fixed. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Problem
The `attributeWithBrackets` test was failing because the `chompBalanced` method in `TokenQueue.java` did not properly handle quoted strings when looking for balanced brackets. When parsing attribute selectors like `div[data='End]']`, the method incorrectly treated the `]` character inside the quoted value `'End]'` as the closing bracket of the attribute selector, instead of looking for the real closing bracket after the complete attribute condition.

## Solution
I modified the `chompBalanced` method to track whether we're inside single or double quotes and only consider brackets for matching when not inside a quoted string. The changes include:

1. Added boolean flags to track when inside single or double quotes
2. Properly toggle the quote state when encountering unescaped quote characters
3. Only process brackets for matching when not inside any quoted string
4. Maintain proper handling of escaped characters

## Verification
- The triggering test `org.jsoup.select.SelectorTest::attributeWithBrackets` now passes
- All project tests pass, indicating no regressions were introduced
- The fix properly handles both single and double-quoted attribute values containing brackets

The resulting patch correctly fixes the issue while maintaining the existing functionality of the `chompBalanced` method.

result:
The bug has been successfully fixed. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Problem
The `attributeWithBrackets` test was failing because the `chompBalanced` method in `TokenQueue.java` did not properly handle quoted strings when looking for balanced brackets. When parsing attribute selectors like `div[data='End]']`, the method incorrectly treated the `]` character inside the quoted value `'End]'` as the closing bracket of the attribute selector, instead of looking for the real closing bracket after the complete attribute condition.

## Solution
I modified the `chompBalanced` method to track whether we're inside single or double quotes and only consider brackets for matching when not inside a quoted string. The changes include:

1. Added boolean flags to track when inside single or double quotes
2. Properly toggle the quote state when encountering unescaped quote characters
3. Only process brackets for matching when not inside any quoted string
4. Maintain proper handling of escaped characters

## Verification
- The triggering test `org.jsoup.select.SelectorTest::attributeWithBrackets` now passes
- All project tests pass, indicating no regressions were introduced
- The fix properly handles both single and double-quoted attribute values containing brackets

The resulting patch correctly fixes the issue while maintaining the existing functionality of the `chompBalanced` method.
